skill.name: domain-name-brainstormer
skill.description: Generates creative domain name ideas for projects, products, and brands. It suggests names across common TLDs and explains branding tradeoffs.
skill.summary: Brainstorms memorable domain names and practical naming options for new projects.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown prose and fenced examples. No executable code, shell commands, credential access, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Prompt Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick locations are Markdown code fences showing user prompt examples and sample output. They do not contain Ruby backtick execution, shell commands, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is documentation for how to ask the assistant for domain ideas. There is no interpreter, command runner, script, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain skill metadata, branding guidance, TLD examples, sample domain names, or workflow text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption code, or security-sensitive cryptographic use appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary English and domain naming examples at the reported locations. There is no code path where weak cryptography could affect confidentiality or integrity.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Naming Advice
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are domain naming tips about pronounceability and avoiding numbers. They do not request host details, environment data, filesystem listings, or other reconnaissance information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is clearly part of a checklist for human-friendly domain names. No system inspection behavior or instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Finding a strong domain name is slow when many good options are already taken. This skill generates focused name ideas, compares TLD choices, and helps users shortlist practical options.
actual_capabilities: Generates domain name ideas from a project, product, brand, or audience description.
Suggests options across TLDs such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, and .tech.
Explains why suggested names may be memorable, descriptive, pronounceable, or brandable.
Provides alternatives when a preferred name or extension may not be available.
Offers naming workflows for startups, personal brands, and product launches.
Adds pricing context for standard, premium, and alternative domain extensions.
limitations: Real availability and pricing can change and should be verified with a registrar.
It does not register domains or purchase names for the user.
It does not provide legal advice or confirm trademark clearance.
Output quality depends on the detail and clarity of the project brief.

use_cases.title: Name a New Startup
use_cases.description: Generate short, brandable domain options across trusted and startup-friendly extensions.

use_cases.title: Choose a Product Domain
use_cases.description: Create descriptive domain options that match a product category, target market, and launch positioning.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Brand
use_cases.description: Explore name-based and profession-based domains for portfolios, consulting sites, or creator projects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Domain Ideas
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting with a simple project description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Brainstorm domain names for a project that helps [audience] do [main task]. Include .com and two alternative TLDs.

prompt_templates.title: Short Brandable Names
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when memorability and brevity matter most.
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest short, brandable domain names for [product]. Prefer names under 12 characters and explain the strongest options.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword-Guided Naming
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the domain should include specific words or themes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create domain name ideas for [business] using themes around [keywords]. Group them by .com, .io, .dev, and .ai.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Naming Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when comparing naming patterns in a market.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze common domain patterns in [industry]. Then suggest differentiated domain names for [product] with rationale, risks, and registrar verification steps.

output_examples.input: A developer tool for sharing code snippets with teammates.
output: snippet.dev: Clear fit for a developer audience and easy to remember.
codeclip.io: Short, action-oriented, and suitable for a collaborative tool.
snippetbox.com: More descriptive and familiar, but should be checked with a registrar.

output_examples.input: A personal finance app for freelancers.
output: freelanceledger.com: Descriptive and credible for money management.
taxflow.app: Strong for a focused tax workflow product.
earnwise.co: Brandable and broad enough for future features.
wearrenew.com: Memorable and directly tied to reuse.
threadcycle.co: Good for circular fashion positioning.
kindfabric.eco: Values-driven, but verify extension pricing and buyer trust.

best_practices: Give the skill a clear audience, product category, and tone before asking for names.
Ask for multiple TLD groups so you can compare trust, price, and availability tradeoffs.
Verify final choices with a registrar, social handle search, and trademark search.
anti_patterns: Do not assume suggested availability is final without real-time registrar confirmation.
Do not choose a name only because the domain is short.
Do not skip trademark review for commercial products or regulated markets.

faq.question: Can this skill confirm that a domain is available?
faq.answer: It can guide availability checks and suggest likely options. Final availability must be verified with a registrar.

faq.question: Which domain extensions does it consider?
faq.answer: It discusses common extensions such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, .xyz, .design, and .tech.

faq.question: Can it help with personal domains?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest name-based, profession-based, and portfolio-friendly domain options.

faq.question: Does it check trademarks?
faq.answer: No. It can remind users to check trademarks, but it does not provide legal clearance.

faq.question: Can it produce multilingual name ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for brainstorming names in English and Spanish when requested.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation only and contains no executable code or malicious instructions.
